g.text: Here all the validations are done in server side , which
means in poor conditions users might wait 10–20
seconds just to discover a simple form validation error.

4. They decided to put java in the browser, but java was too
heavyweight for small page interactions So they decided
to use java as the main language by creating java applets
that would handle heavy tasks like
Game, Chart Tool, Calculator App etc.
And create a lightweight scripting language for the browser
that would be a helper language for handling
Then Brendan Eich( an engineer of netscape) wrote the
prototype in roughly ten days in May 1995.
It was named Livescript first but then changed to JavaScript
to borrow Java’s fame, even though the two languages are
That single business decision “make it look like Java”
is what ruled out simply adopting Python or Perl,
which existed and would have fit.
The best technical choice lost to a marketing requirement.
while JavaScript became rthe primary language of the web.
If Java was so powerful...How JS won?
